title: Building Great Agent Skills: The Missing Manual
summary: The Skill Checklist Framework provides a structured approach to building high-quality agent skills across four dimensions: Trigger (user vs. model-invoked), Structure (steps and references), Steering (leading words and cognitive load), and Pruning (removing legacy and no-op instructions). The framework addresses the practical problem of "skill hell" by establishing a shared rubric for developers building maintainable skills for AI agents.
writeup: This AI Engineer talk presents the Skill Checklist Framework, a structured rubric for building high-quality agent skills across four dimensions: Trigger (user versus model-invoked), Structure (steps and references), Steering (leading words and cognitive load), and Pruning (removing legacy and no-op instructions). The framework targets the practical problem the talk calls "skill hell," giving developers a shared standard for maintainable skills. The takeaways are the four dimensions themselves and the distinction between skills invoked by users and those invoked by the model.
